audit_status.source_base: Greek Revelation text tradition with woman/dragon/child symbolism; draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for woman clothed with sun, dragon, child, heavenly conflict, accuser cast down, wilderness protection.

verses.restored: A great sign was seen in heaven: a woman clothed with sun, moon under her feet, and upon her head crown of twelve stars.
She was pregnant and cries out, travailing and in pain to give birth.
Another sign was seen in heaven: look, a great red dragon, having seven heads and ten horns, and upon his heads seven crowns.
His tail drags a third of stars of heaven and casts them to earth. The dragon stood before woman about to give birth, so that when she bore, he might devour her child.
She bore a male child, who is about to shepherd all nations with iron rod; her child was caught up to the Divine and to his throne.
The woman fled into wilderness, where she has a place prepared by the Divine, so they might nourish her there one thousand two hundred sixty days.
War happened in heaven: Michael and his messengers fought with dragon, and dragon and his messengers fought,
but he did not prevail, nor was place found for them any longer in heaven.
The great dragon was cast down, the ancient serpent, called slanderer and adversarial one, the one misleading whole inhabited world; he was cast to earth, and his messengers with him.

I heard great voice in heaven saying: Now came salvation/healing and power and reign of our Divine and authority of his Messiah, because accuser of our siblings was cast down, the one accusing them before our Divine day and night.
They conquered him through blood/life of Lamb and through word of their witness, and they did not love their life/soul even until death.
Because of this rejoice, heavens and those dwelling in them. Woe to earth and sea, because the slanderer/adversary came down to you having great rage, knowing he has little season.
When dragon saw he was cast to earth, he pursued the woman who bore the male child.
To the woman were given two wings of great eagle, so she might fly into wilderness to her place, where she is nourished a season and seasons and half season, away from face of serpent.
The serpent cast from his mouth water like river after the woman, so that she might be swept away by river.
The earth helped the woman, opening its mouth and swallowing the river that dragon cast from his mouth.
And dragon was enraged against the woman and went away to make war with the rest of her seed, those keeping commands of Divine and holding witness of Jesus.

translation_notes: Woman/child/dragon are symbolic, drawing Israel/Messianic/community imagery; avoid reducing the woman to only one referent.
The accuser is defeated by Lamb-life and witness, not by domination.
Wilderness is protection and nourishment, not abandonment.
safety_note: Do not use dragon/serpent imagery to demonize people. It symbolizes accuser/deceiver power opposed to faithful witness and vulnerable birth.
